title: Introduction to Data Science
author: using a search engine
target_audience: developing high-performance or production code, such as C, C++, or Java, while others are more focused on prototyping code, among these the best known
subject: Data Science
description: This course introduces Data Science, defining it as a methodology for inferring actionable insights from data. It explores the novelty of data science, driven by 'datification' (quantifying previously unquantified aspects of the world) and the 'democratization of data analysis' through accessible computational resources and open-source tools. The curriculum covers four key strategies for data exploration: probing reality, pattern discovery, predicting future events, and understanding people and the world. It also emphasizes the importance of selecting appropriate programming languages and tools, with a focus on Python for its flexibility and extensive ecosystem. Topics include data operations, descriptive statistics, exploratory data analysis, statistical inference, supervised learning (SVM, random forest), regression analysis (linear, multiple, polynomial), unsupervised learning (clustering), and network analysis (graphs, social networks, PageRank).
learning_outcomes: An understanding of the data operations
An overview of simple statistical models and the basics of machine learning techniques of regression.
An understanding good practices of data science
Skills in the use of tools such as python, IDE
Understanding of the basics of the Supervised learning
Describe what Data Science is and the skill sets needed to be a data scientist
Explain the significance of exploratory data analysis (EDA) in data science
Ability to learn the supervised learning, SVM
Apply basic machine learning algorithms (Linear Regression)
